based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/external/pytorch.adam.multi-step.golden.jsonl. v0.9.1 Adam multi-step adversarial plate.

mutation.explanation: Adam hyperparameters {name, beta1, beta2, epsilon, weight_decay} MUST be IDENTICAL across all receipts in a multi-step bundle. learning_rate is EXCLUDED (LR schedules are legitimate). Drifting beta1 from 0.9 to 0.95 at step 1 violates the constancy invariant. Rule 26 catches this — analog of Rule 10 for trace identity. (NOTE: Rule 22 may ALSO fire on step 1 because m_after stored value at step 1 was computed under the original beta1=0.9, not the mutated 0.95.)

reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 26: Optimizer-config constancy — receipts[1].optimizer_config.beta1 (0.95) differs from receipts[0].optimizer_config.beta1 (0.9); hyperparameters must be identical across bundle.
purpose: v0.9.1 Adam multi-step anti-circularity fixture (kind: 'hyperparameter-inconstancy'). Pressure-tests that the named multi-step rule fires BEFORE the reconciler consults fixture_status metadata.
v0_9_1_trust_framing: Adam multi-step rules 25 (state chain) + 26 (config constancy) are STRUCTURAL consistency checks. Producer-authenticity caveat per Rule 17 / Fang et al. 2023 applies.
